gene_description: Beta-1 Adrenergic Receptor — primary target of beta-1 selective blockers (metoprolol, atenolol, bisoprolol). Arg389Gly polymorphism affects receptor signaling capacity and drug response.

source.title: Effect of the Arg389Gly β1-adrenoceptor polymorphism on plasma renin activity and heart rate, and the genotype-dependent response to metoprolol treatment
source.journal: Clinical and Experimental Pharmacology and Physiology
source.finding: ADRB1 rs1801253 Arg389Gly polymorphism predicts metoprolol response. Arg389Arg carriers show greater reductions in systolic/diastolic BP and heart rate compared to Gly389 carriers.

CC.effect: Homozygous for Arg389. Higher beta-1 receptor signaling capacity. Greater hemodynamic response to beta-blockers. Significant reductions in systolic BP (>10 mmHg), diastolic BP, and resting heart rate. Better heart rate control in acute coronary syndromes. Represents ~27-36% of Europeans.
CC.recommendation: Standard beta-blocker dosing expected to be effective. Monitor heart rate and BP response at baseline and 2-4 weeks. May require lower doses to achieve target heart rate/BP due to enhanced responsiveness.

CG.effect: Mixed Arg/Gly alleles. Intermediate beta-1 receptor signaling. Intermediate beta-blocker response. Moderate reductions in BP and heart rate. More variable individual response patterns.
CG.recommendation: Standard beta-blocker dosing with careful monitoring. Assess heart rate and BP response at 2-4 weeks and adjust dose as needed. May benefit from dose titration protocols.

GC.effect: Mixed Arg/Gly alleles. Intermediate beta-1 receptor signaling. Intermediate beta-blocker response. Moderate reductions in BP and heart rate. More variable individual response patterns.
GC.recommendation: Standard beta-blocker dosing with careful monitoring. Assess heart rate and BP response at 2-4 weeks and adjust dose as needed. May benefit from dose titration protocols.

GG.effect: Homozygous for Gly389. Lower beta-1 receptor signaling capacity. Reduced hemodynamic response to beta-blockers. Smaller reductions in BP and heart rate. May require higher doses or higher cumulative dosing to achieve target heart rate/BP. Associated with severe coronary artery stenosis in some populations.
GG.recommendation: May require higher beta-blocker doses to achieve therapeutic BP and heart rate targets. Close monitoring essential — assess response at 2-4 weeks and consider dose escalation. Alternative beta-blockers or add-on agents may be needed if targets not achieved.
